words.def: connected with soldiers or the armed forces
words.example: We may have to take military action.

words.def: a deep hole or holes under the ground where minerals such as coal, gold, etc. are dug
words.example: a copper/diamond mine

words.def: a person who works in a mine taking out coal, gold, diamonds, etc.
words.example: Rescuers are trying to save miners trapped underground after a gas explosion.

words.def: a substance that is naturally present in the earth and is not formed from animal or vegetable matter, for example gold and salt. Some minerals are also present in food and drink and in the human body and are essential for good health.
words.example: a country rich in mineral resources

words.def: the smallest that is possible or allowed; extremely small
words.example: a minimum charge/price

words.def: a senior member of the government who is in charge of a government department or a branch of one
words.example: the Minister of Education

words.def: not very large, important or serious
words.example: The new plan involves widening a minor road through the valley.

words.def: the smaller part of a group; less than half of the people or things in a large group
words.example: Only a small minority of students is/are interested in politics these days.

words.def: very unhappy or uncomfortable
words.example: We were cold, wet and thoroughly miserable.

words.def: that cannot exist or be done; not possible
words.example: almost/virtually/nearly impossible

words.def: an action or an opinion that is not correct, or that produces a result that you did not want
words.example: It's easy to make a mistake.

words.def: a combination of different people or things
words.example: It's a school with a good social and ethnic mix of children.

words.def: consisting of different qualities or elements
words.example: a mixed diet

words.def: a combination of different things
words.example: She was a curious mixture, part grand lady, part wild child.

words.def: a particular way of doing something; a particular type of something
words.example: a mode of communication

words.def: a copy of something, usually smaller than the original object
words.example: a working model (= one in which the parts move) of a fire engine

words.def: not very large, expensive, important, etc.
words.example: modest improvements/reforms

words.def: to change something slightly, especially in order to make it more suitable for a particular purpose
words.example: Patients are taught how to modify their diet.

words.def: a screen that shows information from a computer; a television screen used to show particular kinds of information
words.example: The details of today's flights are displayed on the monitor.

words.def: (in stories) an imaginary creature that is very large, ugly and frightening
words.example: a monster with three heads

words.def: happening once a month or every month
words.example: a monthly meeting/visit/magazine

words.def: a building, column, statue, etc. built to remind people of a famous person or event
words.example: A monument to him was erected in St Paul's Cathedral.

words.def: connected with principles of right and wrong behaviour
words.example: a moral issue/dilemma

words.def: used to introduce some new information that adds to or supports what you have said previously
words.example: A talented artist, he was, moreover, a writer of some note.

words.def: a legal agreement by which a bank or similar organization lends you money to buy a house, etc., and you pay the money back over a particular number of years; the sum of money that you borrow
words.example: to apply for/take out/pay off a mortgage

words.def: a building where Muslims go to worship (= praise God)
words.example: I used to pray at the local mosque with my father and grandfather.

words.def: the act or process of moving or the way something moves
words.example: What was Newton's first law of motion?

words.def: to be the reason why somebody does something or behaves in a particular way
words.example: motivate somebody (to do something), What motivates people to carry out such attacks?

words.def: the reason why somebody does something or behaves in a particular way
words.example: motivation (behind something), What is the motivation behind this sudden change?

words.def: having an engine; using the power of an engine
words.example: The street is closed to motor vehicles.

words.def: to organize and begin something
words.example: Residents mounted a campaign to fight the plans.

words.def: causing strong, often sad, feelings about somebody/something
words.example: a deeply moving experience

words.def: many in number; involving many different people or things
words.example: The shape appears multiple times within each painting.

words.def: to add a number to itself a particular number of times
words.example: The children are already learning to multiply and divide.

words.def: difficult to understand or explain; strange
words.example: He died in mysterious circumstances.

words.def: a story from ancient times, especially one that was told to explain natural events or to describe the early history of a people; this type of story
words.example: ancient Greek myths

words.def: not wearing any clothes
words.example: She was clutching the sheet around her naked body.

words.def: measuring a short distance from one side to the other, especially in relation to length
words.example: Stray dogs wander the steep narrow lanes of the old town.

words.def: very bad or unpleasant
words.example: He had a nasty accident.

words.def: connected with a particular nation; shared by a whole nation
words.example: Decide whether it would be better to advertise in a national or a local newspaper.

words.def: the skill or the process of planning a route for a ship or other vehicle and taking it there
words.example: a maker of in-car navigation systems

words.def: near in position; not far away
words.example: Her mother lived in a nearby town.

words.def: tidy and in order; carefully done or arranged
words.example: She kept her desk extremely neat.

words.def: used to say that something cannot be avoided
words.example: The number of places available is necessarily limited.

words.def: the fact that something must happen or be done; the need for something
words.example: necessity for something, We recognize the necessity for a written agreement.

words.def: bad or harmful
words.example: The crisis had a negative effect on trade.

words.def: to try to reach an agreement by formal discussion
words.example: negotiate (with somebody) (for something), The government will not negotiate with terrorists.

words.def: formal discussion between people who are trying to reach an agreement
words.example: peace/trade/contract negotiations

words.def: used to show that a negative statement is also true of somebody/something else
words.example: He didn't remember and neither did I.

words.def: any of the long fibres that carry messages between the brain and parts of the body, enabling you to move, feel pain, etc.
words.example: the optic nerve

words.def: not supporting or helping either side in a disagreement, competition, etc.
words.example: Journalists are supposed to be politically neutral.

words.def: despite something that you have just mentioned
words.example: There is little chance that we will succeed in changing the law. Nevertheless, it is important that we try.

words.def: recently
words.example: a newly qualified doctor

words.def: a dream that is very frightening or unpleasant
words.example: He still has nightmares about the accident.
